message:
Your dog drops their head mid-walk and starts chomping grass like a tiny, contented cow — and you immediately think, "Uh oh, are they sick?" 🐾

Good news: probably not. The old belief that dogs eat grass to force themselves to throw up doesn't really hold up. When researchers looked into it, fewer than 1 in 4 dogs actually vomited afterward, and only around 1 in 10 seemed unwell beforehand. For most dogs, nibbling grass is just… normal dog stuff. It may add a little fiber, it's an instinct passed down from their wild ancestors, or honestly, they might just like the taste and the fresh-air snack.

So a few mouthfuls of grass usually isn't a red flag — it's a dog being a dog. (Just make sure the lawn they're grazing on is free of pesticides and weed killers, and check in with your vet if the munching suddenly becomes nonstop.) 💛

Is your dog a grass grazer? What's their favorite spot to snack? 🌱🐶
